## Choose a PR template

GitHub does not show a template picker for pull requests like it does for issues. Use one of the links below **on the “Open a pull request” page** (after you select base and compare branches). That reloads the form with the right template.

If a link does not apply the template in your browser, add `?expand=1&template=<name>.md` to the URL bar, or copy the body from [`.github/PULL_REQUEST_TEMPLATE/`](.github/PULL_REQUEST_TEMPLATE/).

| Type | Open this PR with the template |
|------|--------------------------------|
| Bugfix | [bugfix.md](?expand=1&template=bugfix.md) |
| Feature | [feature.md](?expand=1&template=feature.md) |
| Refactor | [refactor.md](?expand=1&template=refactor.md) |
| Docs | [docs.md](?expand=1&template=docs.md) |
| Chore | [chore.md](?expand=1&template=chore.md) |

Click a link **before** writing the description; the page reloads with that template in the PR body. If the URL already contains `?`, append `&expand=1&template=<name>.md` instead of starting with `?`.
